briefdescription.para: The static initializer used to initialize an atomic_flag to the clear state.
The static initializer for an atomic object of a type that is initialization-compatible with value.
Initializes the atomic object at obj with the value value.

detaileddescription.para: Note that this function does not avoid data races.

briefdescription.para: Terminates a dependency chain; the argument does not carry a dependency to the return value.
Indicates the lock-free property of integer and address atomic types.

simplesect.para: 0 if the type is never lock-free, 1 if the type is sometimes lock-free, and 2 if the type is always lock-free.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_store_explicit(object, desired, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with the value of desired.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_load_explicit(object, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically returns the value at object.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_exchange_explicit(object, desired, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with desired.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_compare_exchange_strong_explicit(object, expected, desired, memory_order_seq_cst, memory_order_seq_cst).

Atomically compares the value at object for equality with that at expected, and if true, replaces the value at object with desired, and if false, updates the value at expected with the value at object.

detaileddescription.para: Further, if the comparison is true, memory is affected according to the value of success, and if the comparison is false, memory is affected according to the value of failure.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_compare_exchange_weak_explicit(object, expected, desired, memory_order_seq_cst, memory_order_seq_cst).
Equivalent to atomic_compare_exchange_strong_explicit(), except that a weak compare-and-exchange may fail spuriously.

detaileddescription.para: That is, even when the contents of memory referred to by expected and object are equal, it may return zero and store back to expected the same memory contents that were there before.

The spurious failure enables implementation of compare-and-exchange on a broader class of machines. When a compare-and-exchange is in a loop, the weak version will yield better performance on some platforms. When a weak compare-and-exchange would require a loop and a strong one would not, the strong one is preferable.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_fetch_add_explicit(object, operand, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with *object + operand.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_fetch_sub_explicit(object, operand, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with *object - operand.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_fetch_or_explicit(object, operand, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with *object | operand.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_fetch_xor_explicit(object, operand, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with *object ^ operand.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_fetch_and_explicit(object, operand, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically replaces the value at object with *object & operand.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: No operation orders memory.
A load operation performs a consume operation on the affected memory location.
A load operation performs an acquire operation on the affected memory location.
A store operation performs a release operation on the affected memory location.
A load operation performs an acquire operation on the affected memory location, and a store operation performs a release operation on the affected memory location.
Enforces a single total order on all affected locations.
An enumerated type identifying memory constraints.
Inserts a fence with semantics according to order.
Equivalent to atomic_thread_fence(order), except that the resulting ordering constraints are established only between a thread and a signal handler executing in the same thread.
Atomically sets the value at object to true.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

simplesect.para: the value at object immediately before the effects.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_flag_test_and_set_explicit(object, memory_order_seq_cst).
Atomically sets the value at object to false.

detaileddescription.para: Memory is affected according to order.

briefdescription.para: Equivalent to atomic_flag_test_and_set_explicit(object, memory_order_seq_cst).
This header file is part of the C11 and POSIX compatibility library; it includes <stdatomic.h>, if it exists, and defines any missing functionality.

detaileddescription.para:
